mechanism_narrative: Sustained decline in the federal funds rate below 0.5% signals monetary accommodation that reduces equity discount rates and increases present value of future corporate earnings, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 0.12% as of May 2021 indicates deep accommodation
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns when FEDFUNDS < 0.5% are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month payroll employment growth above 500 thousand signals robust labor market recovery that sustains consumer spending and corporate earnings, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS month-over-month growth exceeds 500 thousand persons
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS at 142.9 million as of May 2021 reflects post-pandemic recovery
regime_hint: post-shock recovery with strong labor demand
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 60-day returns in > 55% of triggered instances despite strong payroll growth, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Rising consumer spending measured by retail sales growth combined with accelerating housing starts indicates demand-driven expansion that supports equity valuations, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS month-over-month growth > 1.5% AND HOUST month-over-month growth > 2%
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 448.1 billion and HOUST at 1.06 million as of April 2021
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with synchronized demand acceleration
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in > 60% of instances where both RSAFS and HOUST growth thresholds are met, or joint signal Rank IC < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production index declining month-over-month signals supply-side constraints or demand weakness that compresses profit margins and equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
regime_hint: supply shock or demand contraction regime
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in > 65% of instances where INDPRO declines exceed -0.5%, or Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Unemployment rate rising above 5.5% signals labor market deterioration that reduces consumer confidence and spending, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
regime_hint: labor market deterioration or recession onset
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 60% of instances where UNRATE > 5.5%, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Inflation measured by CPI accelerating above 3% year-over-year combined with federal funds rate remaining below 1% signals monetary policy lag that creates financial instability expectations, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL at 238.11 and FEDFUNDS at 0.12% as of May 2021
regime_hint: monetary policy lag with inflation acceleration
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in > 55% of instances where both inflation and rate conditions are met, or joint signal Rank IC < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth measured quarterly at rates above 2% annualized signals sustained economic expansion that supports corporate earnings and equity valuations, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 at 16417.8 billion chained 2017 dollars as of Q1 2021
regime_hint: any regime with positive real growth
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 60-day returns in > 55% of quarters where GDPC1 growth exceeds 2% annualized, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production growth combined with declining unemployment rate signals supply-side expansion and tight labor markets that support wage growth and corporate profitability, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO month-over-month growth > 0.8% AND UNRATE month-over-month change < -0.2pp
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 107.21 and UNRATE at 5.0% as of April-May 2021
regime_hint: synchronized supply and labor market tightening
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in > 60% of instances where both INDPRO and UNRATE conditions are met, or joint signal Rank IC < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window.
